title: Prime Day Keeps on Truckin', and We're Here Tracking the Deals and Duds
gist: Amazon Prime Day day two live blog from WIRED covering a wide range of consumer electronics and home goods deals. Notable for the Apple Watch Series 11 at $279 (30% off, a 6-hour battery life improvement over prior generation cited as meaningful), the Jackery Explorer 1000 at 50% off, and editor commentary on product pricing dynamics.

insights.claim: The Apple Watch Series 11 sold for $279 (30% off from $399) during Prime Day, with WIRED editors noting its 24-hour battery life—a 6-hour improvement over predecessor models—makes it suitable for sleep tracking for the first time, and describing the improvement as 'THIS CHANGES EVERYTHING.'
insights.speaker: Matthew Korfhage
insights.so_what: Battery life crossing the 24-hour threshold unlocks sleep tracking as a genuine use case for Apple Watch, expanding health data capture and potential revenue from health services and partnerships.
insights.quote: The Series 11 is the best smartwatch for iPhone users in part because of its 24-hour battery life, which is a 6-hour improvement over previous models.

insights.claim: Form's Smart Swim 2 LT Goggles with an embedded heads-up display were reviewed and placed at $134 ($15 off, lowest price ever), with WIRED editors noting that while smart glasses on land are seen as 'weird and creepy,' there is a practical application in the water where checking a watch mid-swim is not possible.
insights.speaker: Martin Cizmar
insights.so_what: The framing of smart glasses as socially acceptable only in specific use-case environments (underwater, AR industry settings) is relevant to smart glasses market sizing—consumer resistance to visible wearables remains a product adoption constraint.
insights.quote: While I personally (along with literally everyone I know in the real world), would not be caught dead wearing weird, creepy, and useless smart glasses on land, there is a practical application in the water

insights.claim: The Fitbit Charge 6, three years old, remained WIRED's recommended Fitbit at $85 (47% off) during Prime Day, with editors noting it 'may remain so until full Googlefication of the line in the next year'—explicitly flagging an anticipated product line transition.
insights.speaker: Scott Gilbertson
insights.so_what: Google's anticipated integration of Fitbit into its Pixel ecosystem could accelerate Wear OS adoption and strengthen Google's health data platform—watch for product announcements in the next 12 months.
insights.quote: the Charge 6 remains our favorite Fitbit and may remain so until full Googlfication of the line in the next year
